---
title: "Edit your menu"
description: "Change names, prices, descriptions, photos, modifiers, and more from the vendor workspace."
sidebarTitle: "Edit your menu"
icon: "pencil"
---

The menu editor is where you shape what diners see. It's organised as one
scrollable carousel of tabs — everything you need is one swipe away.

## Where it lives

Your workspace menu editor is at `rumizi.com/your-venue/w/menu/{menu-id}`.
Open it from **Menus** in the workspace and pick the menu you want to edit.

## The tabs, in order

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Items">
    Your main editing surface. Each row is an item with its name, price,
    description, tags, and photo. You can:

    - Change names, prices, and descriptions.
    - Add a photo (upload, pick from the gallery, or generate with AI).
    - Add modifiers — sizes, extras, spice level, anything with a choice.
    - Mark an item as **Out of stock** with a return time.
    - Pin to the **Popular** carousel that diners see first.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Categories">
    Group items into sections like *Starters*, *Mains*, *Desserts*. You
    can drag to reorder, add a category photo, and write a short
    description that appears above the items.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Modifiers">
    Build reusable option groups — *Size: S/M/L*, *Spice level*, *Milk
    choice*. Attach a group to any item or category.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Cross-sell">
    Suggest complementary items on the item detail page. For example,
    pair *Margherita pizza* with *Sparkling water*. Pairs are
    directional, so you can suggest the drink from the pizza without
    suggesting the pizza from the drink.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Schedule">
    Set the hours this menu is available. See
    [Open hours](/vendors/menu/scheduling).
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Translations">
    Add translated names and descriptions for each language you support.
    See [Translations](/vendors/menu/translations).
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Basics">
    Menu name, description, visual theme, currency, and the AI
    assistant context for this menu. See
    [Venue theme](/vendors/venue/theme).
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Saving works across tabs

You can edit Items, swipe to Schedule, edit there, swipe to Translations,
and keep going. Rumizi remembers what's unsaved on every tab.

When you leave the editor, refresh, or tap the browser back button, you're
asked whether to **save all**, **discard all**, or jump to the first tab
with unsaved changes. You won't lose work by accident.

<Tip>
The **Save all** button in the editor header saves every dirty tab in one
click.
</Tip>

## Item availability — the "86" flow

When something runs out, open the item and pick an availability mode:

- **In stock** — available now.
- **Out until you change it** — indefinite.
- **Out until tomorrow** — comes back at midnight in your venue's
  timezone.
- **Out until next opening** — comes back when your next scheduled menu
  window starts (needs [open hours](/vendors/menu/scheduling)).

Out-of-stock changes are live immediately — you don't need to republish.

## Featured items (the Popular carousel)

Toggle **Featured** on an item to pin it to the **Popular** row that shows
at the top of the menu. It's how you highlight the chef's picks, the new
dish, or whatever you want diners to try first.

## Photos

Items with photos convert better. You can:

- Upload a photo from your phone or computer.
- Pick an existing image from the [gallery](/vendors/media/gallery).
- Generate one with AI from the item name and description — see
  [AI images](/vendors/media/ai-images).
- Improve an existing photo with AI (remove clutter, brighten, recompose).

## Publishing what you edited

<Warning>
Edits are hidden from diners until you publish. If you want to preview what
a change looks like from a diner's point of view first, see
[Publish and preview](/vendors/menu/publish).
</Warning>
